description: A lightweight assisted rule inspired by D&D 5e SRD: 12 classes, proficiency bonus, d20 checks, and simplified combat; suited for narrative play, not a full RAW recreation.
attr_hint: D&D attributes use 3-20 values; 10 is roughly average for a commoner, and even numbers usually yield better d20 modifiers.
skill_hint: D&D skills are closer to "proficiencies" or class abilities. You may write favored skills or class ability names, values optional; proficiency bonus is mostly determined by level. Roll cheat sheet: advantage = roll 2d20 take higher, disadvantage = take lower; if both apply, they cancel out.

classes.name: Barbarian
classes.description: A furious melee warrior whose rage is their weapon

classes.name: Bard
classes.description: An artist who weaves magic through music and words

classes.name: Cleric
classes.description: A divine caster serving the gods, healing the wounded and turning undead

classes.name: Druid
classes.description: Guardian of nature's power, able to shapeshift into beasts

classes.name: Fighter
classes.description: A tactical master proficient with all weapons and armor

classes.name: Monk
classes.description: An ascetic whose body is a weapon, master of unarmed combat

classes.name: Paladin
classes.description: A heavy-armored knight bound by sacred oaths, punishing evil and protecting the innocent

classes.name: Ranger
classes.description: A wilderness hunter and tracker, skilled with ranged weapons and two-weapon combat

classes.name: Rogue
classes.description: A skulker in shadows, expert at lockpicking and sneak attacks

classes.name: Sorcerer
classes.description: An innate caster with magic flowing through their bloodline

classes.name: Warlock
classes.description: Gains dark power through a pact with an otherworldly patron

classes.name: Wizard
classes.description: An academic caster who masters arcana through years of study

gm_prompt_appendix: You run Dungeons & Dragons 5th edition rules. Characters have a proficiency bonus (proficiency_bonus = 2 + (level-1)//4); check format: (Strength check: d20 + ability modifier + proficiency bonus vs DC). Advantage/disadvantage follow D&D 5e: advantage rolls 2d20 take the higher, disadvantage rolls 2d20 take the lower; when both apply they cancel and do not stack. In combat: an attack roll determines a hit (d20 + Strength/Dexterity modifier + proficiency bonus vs AC = 10 + Dexterity modifier + armor); on a hit, roll damage dice + ability modifier. Each round every player may move 30 feet and take 1 action or 1 bonus action. Casters expend spell slots (a 1st-level character has two 1st-level slots). A short rest restores some HP (roll a Hit Die + Constitution modifier); a long rest fully restores HP and recovers spell slots.

difficulty_instructions.轻松: Monster CR 1 below party level, DC -2, plentiful loot
difficulty_instructions.标准: Monster CR equals party level, DC as normal, reasonable loot
difficulty_instructions.硬核: Monster CR 1-2 above party level, DC +2, scarce resources
